title: cheirolophus intybaceus subsp. lagunae
intro: Cheirolophus intybaceus subsp. lagunae is a plant species in the daisy family, native to the Canary Islands, and is also known as the Hierba de las Lagunas or Lagunas Daisy. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. Cheirolophus intybaceus subsp. lagunae is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and helping to lower blood pressure. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
